_source: CPIC Guideline for efavirenz and CYP2B6 (PMID: 29034817, updated 2021)
_equity_context: CYP2B6 poor metabolizers are disproportionately represented in African populations due to *6 (rs3745274) and *18 (rs28399499) allele frequencies. Standard efavirenz dosing (600mg/day) leads to toxic plasma levels in CYP2B6 PMs, causing CNS adverse effects and increasing ART discontinuation in sub-Saharan African patients.

efavirenz.Rapid Metabolizer: Consider higher dose (800mg/day) or alternative ARV if subtherapeutic levels observed. Monitor viral suppression.
efavirenz.Normal Metabolizer: Initiate at standard recommended dose (600 mg/day). No dose adjustment required based on genotype alone.
efavirenz.Intermediate Metabolizer: Initiate at standard dose. Consider monitoring plasma efavirenz concentrations to confirm therapeutic levels (1-4 mg/L).
efavirenz.Poor Metabolizer: STRONGLY CONSIDER alternative antiretroviral (dolutegravir, rilpivirine). If efavirenz is used, reduce to 400 mg/day and monitor plasma levels. CNS toxicity (nightmares, dizziness, depression) is significantly elevated. Risk of treatment discontinuation in African patients.

bupropion.Normal Metabolizer: Initiate at standard dose (150-300 mg/day). Standard therapeutic response expected.
bupropion.Intermediate Metabolizer: Standard dosing. Monitor for therapeutic response and tolerability.
bupropion.Poor Metabolizer: Elevated bupropion and hydroxybupropion plasma levels. Initiate at lower dose (150 mg/day). Monitor for increased seizure risk and cardiovascular effects.
bupropion.Rapid Metabolizer: Reduced drug exposure. May require higher doses for therapeutic effect. Monitor depression symptom response.

cyclophosphamide.Normal Metabolizer: Standard activation rate. Use recommended dose.
cyclophosphamide.Poor Metabolizer: Reduced conversion to active metabolite (4-hydroxycyclophosphamide). Potential for reduced efficacy in oncology. Consider alternative alkylating agent or dose escalation with monitoring.
